summary: Package tarball ships live npm publish token for @arbocollab scope
details: The published tarball contains `npmjs.npmrc` with a live npm registry auth token (`npm_NQgd5Dlw...`) scoped to `@arbocollab`. Anyone who installs or downloads this package obtains a credential that grants publish privileges on every `@arbocollab/*` package in the npm registry. An attacker can use this token to push trojaned versions of arbo-web-people and any sibling @arbocollab/* package, attacking every downstream consumer of the scope. The library code itself (a Vue/Pinia UI bundle pointing at api.tessr.us) declares no lifecycle hooks and shows no installer-side exfiltration or remote code fetch — the supply-chain risk comes entirely from the leaked publish credential being available to any installer. This goes beyond author self-harm because the token can be used to attack other consumers of the @arbocollab scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
